name: Arga River Trail and Alto Hundido Loop
name_original: Sendero Fluvial del Arga – circular por Alto Hundido desde Miranda de Arga
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile Sendero Fluvial del Arga circular route, exploring river landscapes and historical irrigation systems near Mirand
region_intro: Hiking around Miranda De Arga offers a variety of trails through the region's diverse landscape. The area features gentle hills, riverine paths along the Arga River, and routes that pass by historical sites. The terrain includes open fields, some forested sections, and elevated viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Miranda De Arga The most popular hiking route is Senda del Ángel – circular desde Miranda de Arga, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes ascents to elevated points. Another top favourite among local hikers is Montealto – circular by Jenariz from Miranda de Arga, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This route offers views from higher ground and passes through varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Ermita Virgen del Castillo to Alto Cabezon – circular from Miranda de Arga, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through hills and past a hermitage, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Miranda De Arga is defined by river trails, gentle hills, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Miranda De Arga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Miranda De Arga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Miranda De Arga?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Miranda De Arga, offering a variety of experiences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 40 times, contributing to an average rating of 4.4 stars.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes in Miranda De Arga?
A: The region features diverse terrain, including gentle hills, riverine paths along the Arga River, and routes that pass by historical sites. You'll encounter open fields, some forested sections, and elevated viewpoints offering scenic panoramas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Miranda De Arga?
A: Yes, Miranda De Arga offers &lt;strong&gt;7 easy hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt;. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106238562/ermita-virgen-del-castillo-to-alto-cabezon-circular-from-miranda-de-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Virgen del Castillo to Alto Cabezon – circular from Miranda de Arga&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.1 km) long and leads through hills and past a hermitage.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Miranda De Arga?
A: The trails in Miranda De Arga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the peaceful river paths, and the opportunities to discover historical points of interest along the way.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Miranda De Arga?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Miranda De Arga are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106310837/senda-del-angel-circular-desde-miranda-de-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ángel – circular desde Miranda de Arga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105386542/montealto-circular-by-jenariz-from-miranda-de-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealto – circular by Jenariz from Miranda de Arga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking around Miranda De Arga?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2704481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Olite&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6250557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artajona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Miranda De Arga suitable for dogs?
A: Many of the trails in Miranda De Arga, especially those through open fields and along river paths, are generally suitable for dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in this area?
A: Hikes in Miranda De Arga vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106310837/senda-del-angel-circular-desde-miranda-de-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ángel – circular desde Miranda de Arga&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours 32 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105415053/arga-river-trail-and-alto-hundido-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arga River Trail and Alto Hundido Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 6.4 miles (10.2 km), typically takes around 2 hours 45 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Arga River?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the scenic Arga River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105415053/arga-river-trail-and-alto-hundido-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arga River Trail and Alto Hundido Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering pleasant paths alongside the river and through varied landscapes.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Miranda De Arga during autumn?
A: During autumn, temperatures can vary. It's advisable to wear layers, including a waterproof or windproof outer shell, as well as comfortable hiking boots suitable for potentially muddy or uneven terrain. The region's open fields can be exposed to wind.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, Miranda De Arga and nearby towns offer various establishments where you can find refreshments. It's a good idea to check local listings for options before or after your hike.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available in Miranda De Arga itself, often near the starting points of popular trails or in the town center. For routes starting outside the main town, look for designated parking areas or roadside spots, ensuring you park responsibly and legally.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sendero Fluvial del Arga – circular por Alto Hundido desde Miranda de Arga?
faqs.answer: This moderate hiking route usually takes around 2 hours and 45 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate hiking. It requires good physical condition and steady footing, making it suitable for individuals with a reasonable level of fitness rather than complete beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a diverse terrain, including approximately 5.97 km of unpaved tracks, 2.45 km of paved tracks, and about 973 meters of gravel paths. You'll experience a mix of surfaces as you follow the river landscape.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich river landscape with local flora and fauna, historical and modern irrigation infrastructures like the Old Irrigation of Larraga and Berbinzana, and the Navarre Canal. Key viewpoints include the Sunk Summit, offering panoramic views, and the Clock Tower. The route also showcases agricultural landscapes with various crops.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its river landscape and agricultural elements, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery, including flora and agricultural fields, is often at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero Fluvial del Arga – circular por Alto Hundido desde Miranda de Arga dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through agricultural areas and natural habitats, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and respect local farming activities.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is part of a public project aimed at improving access to the paths along the Arga River.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park for this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Miranda de Arga. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Miranda de Arga, which serves as the access point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a camera to capture the scenic river and agricultural views. Given the diverse terrain, sturdy footwear is advisable.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local paths. You will briefly pass through parts of the Senda Las Canales de Avelino, Tirujón, Senda la follaza, and the broader Sendero del Arga.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts from Miranda de Arga, you can find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms within the town before or after your hike.
